No Poverty.reason: The course focuses on corporate financial accounting, not directly addressing poverty eradication, social protection, or economic resource access for the poor. The initial link was too indirect.

Zero Hunger.reason: No evidence in the course description or topics directly or indirectly relates to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course content does not contain any explicit or implicit links to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare services.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (4.3) and professional skills for employment (4.4). Explicit mention of 'sustainability issues' in core competencies aligns with education for sustainable development (4.7).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Financial accounting is crucial for enterprises to access financial services (9.3). The 'sustainability issues' competence and 'Natural Resources' topic link to making industries sustainable (9.4).
